title: Warm Mexican Yam Soup
description: A velvety, savory soup that blends the earthy sweetness of white yam with smoky poblano peppers, finished with a bright lime twist and fresh cilantro.
instructions: Toss sliced poblano peppers with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper, and roast on a baking sheet for 15 minutes until blistered and softened.
While peppers roast, heat the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion and minced garlic, sauté until fragrant and translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in diced white yam, roasted poblano, and vegetable broth, bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 18 minutes, or until yam is tender.
Remove from heat, and blend the soup with an immersion blender until smooth, then return to pot.
Stir in lime juice and chopped cilantro, season with salt and pepper to taste; serve hot.
